title: Identify the error

content.problemStatement: Identify and correct the error in the following code
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (0 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the number of letters in the English representation of the given number (British usage, spaces omitted).

sampleTestCases.explanation: "nineteen" is written as "nineteen" (without spaces) which has 8 letters.
"one hundred and twenty three" without spaces is "onehundredandtwentythree" – 24 letters.

solution.approach: Pre‑define the word fragments for ones, teens and tens. For a given number n we build its British English representation without spaces:  • 0 → "zero"  • 1‑9 → ONES[n]  • 10‑19 → TEENS[n‑10]  • 20‑99 → TENS[tens] + ONES[ones]  • 100‑999 → ONES[hundreds] + "hundred" + (if remainder>0 then "and" + representation of remainder)  • 1000 → "onethousand". The length of the concatenated string is the answer. The algorithm works in O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case.
